title: pastinaca freyniana
intro: Pastinaca freyniana, also known as wild parsnip, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Eastern Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, inflammation, and as a diuretic. The health benefits of Pastinaca freyniana include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its root and leaves can be used in various herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions.
